rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical selenocyclization of N-allyl-N'-tosylureas with PhSeSePh to form 2-amino-oxazoline products bearing a CH2SePh group
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh generates electrophilic PhSe+ (or PhSe radical/PhSe+ equivalent) that adds to the allyl alkene, triggering intramolecular cyclization by the urea oxygen

key_intermediates.name: PhSe electrophile (from anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide)

electron_flow: PhSeSePh is oxidized at the carbon anode to a PhSe+ electrophile (with reduction of protons at the Pt cathode); PhSe+ activates the allyl C=C as a seleniranium ion, which is opened by intramolecular attack of the urea carbonyl oxygen to give the 2-iminooxazoline 3a.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeded under standard conditions with TEMPO (1 equiv.) giving 3a in 80% yield, indicating no free C-radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: 3a obtained in 79% (1 equiv. BHT) and 81% (0.5 equiv. BHT), again consistent with a non-radical (ionic) pathway

notes: Radical-trap experiments (TEMPO, BHT) do not inhibit the reaction, arguing against a free-radical chain and favoring an ionic seleniranium/cation-initiated cyclization following anodic generation of PhSe+. Optimal conditions: C(+)|Pt(-) undivided cell, n-Bu4NBF4, MeCN/H2O (7:1 mL), 10 mA, r.t., 2 h.
